title: klasea flavescens subsp. neglecta
intro: Klasea flavescens subsp. neglecta, commonly known as  a plant species, is a type of flowering plant in the Boraginaceae family. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine and is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Klasea flavescens subsp. neglecta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic actions, such as wound healing, anti-cancer, and anti-diarrheal properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand the effects and potential uses of this plant, and it should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
benefits: Klasea flavescens subsp. neglecta has many benefits, such as its potential to improve cardiovascular health by reducing cholesterol levels and blood pressure, thereby decreasing the risk of heart disease. Additionally, this plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with conditions like arthritis, while also exhibiting antimicrobial properties to combat infections and promote wound healing. Klasea flavescens subsp. neglecta has also been studied for its antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, which can help protect cells from damage and reduce the risk of certain types of cancer. By incorporating this plant into one's lifestyle, individuals may experience improved overall health, increased energy levels, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases, ultimately leading to a longer and healthier life.

constituents: Klasea flavescens subsp. neglecta has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, terpenoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, including kaempferol, quercetin, and isorhapontigenin, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. side_effects: Klasea flavescens subsp. neglecta can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal irritation, nausea, and vomiting, which can be severe if ingested in large quantities. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also cause headaches, dizziness, and respiratory issues. In rare cases, improper use of the plant can lead to allergic reactions, such as skin rashes, itching, and swelling. If ingested or inhaled, Klasea flavescens subsp. neglecta can cause cardiac problems and even seizures in severe cases.
